title: Optimal Color Arrangement under Frequency Constraints

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers `freq` representing the frequency of each color and an integer `n` representing the number of balls, return the maximum number of colors that can be arranged such that no two balls of the same color are adjacent.
statement.inputFormat:
Input is provided through two lines in the format `n
freq0 freq1 freq2 ...`
statement.outputFormat: Output is an integer representing the maximum number of colors that can be arranged
statement.notes: You can assume that `n` and `freq` are non-negative integers.

examples.explanation: Maximum colors that can be arranged with 10 balls and given frequencies.

editorial.title: Optimal Color Arrangement under Frequency Constraints
editorial.intuition: Greedy strategy is to always pick the color with highest frequency and place the balls of that color at least two positions apart.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force solution would be to generate all possible color arrangements and check if no two balls of the same color are adjacent.
editorial.betterSolution: Greedy strategy works because the frequencies array is sorted in descending order.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a greedy strategy with two pointers to place the balls of each color.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Proof of correctness can be achieved by contradiction.
editorial.dryRun: Example dry run to understand the optimal solution.
commonMistakes: Not considering the possibility of two balls of different colors being adjacent

functionSignature.java: public int solve(int[] freq, int n);
functionSignature.python: def solve(self, freq: list[int], n: int) -> int:
